* **CDD_FSI_TX_UNINIT** - Tx module has not initialized yet 
<br> 
  
* **CDD_FSI_TX_INIT** - Tx module has initialized 
<br> 
  
* **CDD_FSI_TX_IDLE** - Transmission through the Tx channel hasn't started 
<br> 
  
* **CDD_FSI_TX_BUSY** - Transmission has been started and is still going on 
<br>

| CddI2cConf_CddI2cChannel_CddI2cChannel_23   | Symbolic Name for I2c Channel 23.  |

### enum Cdd_IpcStatusType
| Enumerator | Value | Description |
| ---------- | ----- | ----------- |
| CDD_IPC_UNINIT | 0U| The Complex Driver is not initialized or not usable.   |
| CDD_IPC_IDLE | 1U| The Complex Driver is not currently writing.   |
| CDD_IPC_ALLOW_ACCESS | 2U| The Complex Driver is currently open.   |
| CDD_IPC_BUSY | 3U| The Complex Driver is performing a Write.   |
| CDD_IPC_CLOSE | 4U| The Complex Driver is currently open.   |

**Brief**: 
This type defines a range of specific status for Complex driver. 

Requirements : AUTORADAR_MCAL-70

Reentrancy - Reentrant

* **None**

### function Cdd_Ipc_Init

    const Cdd_IpcConfigType * ConfigPtr
)

* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None 

### function Cdd_Ipc_DeInit

```
void Cdd_Ipc_DeInit(
    const Cdd_IpcConfigType * ConfigPtr
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service for deinitializing CDD IPC. 

Service for deinitializing the CDD IPC and change driver state to uninitialized

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Non Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **ConfigPtr** - Pointer to configuration set 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None 

### function Cdd_Ipc_Notify_RegisterClient

```
Std_ReturnType Cdd_Ipc_Notify_RegisterClient(
    uint32 Cdd_Ipc_localClientId,
    IpcNotify_FxnCallback Cdd_Ipc_notify_msgHandler,
    void * args
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service to register a client for IPC. 

Service to register client at a specified client ID.

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **Cdd_Ipc_localClientId** - Local core client ID 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_notify_msgHandler** - Pointer to the function that handles the Ipc notify message arrives on that above client ID 

**Returns**: 

  
* **E_OK** 
<br> 
  
* **E_NOT_OK** 
<br> 

**Return**: Std_ReturnType 

### function Cdd_Ipc_Notify_Write

```
Std_ReturnType Cdd_Ipc_Notify_Write(
    uint32 Cdd_Ipc_remoteCoreId,
    uint16 Cdd_Ipc_remoteClientId,
    uint32 Cdd_Ipc_msgValue,
    uint32 Cdd_Ipc_waitForFifoNotFull
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service to write ipc notify message. 

Service to Write message from the IPC instance.

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **Cdd_Ipc_remoteCoreId** - Core ID to which you need to sent the message to 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_remoteClientId** - Remote client ID registered for the to send the message to 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_msgValue** - Message value to be sent 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_waitForFifoNotFull** - 1: wait for message to be inserted into HW or SW FIFO, 0: if FIFO is full, dont send message and return with error. 

**Returns**: 

  
* **E_OK** 
<br> 
  
* **E_NOT_OK** 
<br> 

**Return**: Std_ReturnType

### function Cdd_Ipc_RpMsg_SendMsg

```
Std_ReturnType Cdd_Ipc_RpMsg_SendMsg(
    void * data,
    uint16 dataLen,
    uint16 Cdd_Ipc_remoteCoreId,
    uint16 Cdd_Ipc_remoteEndPt,
    uint16 Cdd_Ipc_localEndPtId,
    uint32 timeout
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service for CDD Ipc RpMsg GetLoclEndPoint. 

Service to Send a message to a remote core at a specified remote end point

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Non-Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **dataLen** – Length of buffer 
  
* **data** – pointer to data buffer 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_remoteCoreId** - Core ID to which you need to send the message to. 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_remoteEndPt** – Unique end point value on the remote core. 
  
* **timeout** – Time in system ticks to block for a message receive. 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_localEndPtId** - localEndPt, is strictly not needed, however this is available to the user on the remote core  * and can be used as a reply end point 

**Returns**: 

  
* **E_OK** 
<br> 
  
* **E_NOT_OK** 
<br> 

**Return**: Std_ReturnType 

### function Cdd_Ipc_RpMsg_RecvMsg

```
Std_ReturnType Cdd_Ipc_RpMsg_RecvMsg(
    void * data,
    uint16 * dataLen,
    uint16 Cdd_Ipc_localEndPtId,
    uint16 * Cdd_Ipc_remoteCoreId,
    uint16 * Cdd_Ipc_remoteEndPt,
    uint32 timeout
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service for CDD Ipc RpMsg GetLoclEndPoint. 

Blocking API to wait till a message is received from any CPU at the specified local end point

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Non-Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **dataLen** – Length of buffer 
  
* **data** – pointer to data buffer 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_remoteCoreId** - Core ID to which you need to send the message to. 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_remoteEndPt** – Pointer to Unique end point value on the remote core. 
  
* **timeout** – Time in system ticks to block for a message receive. 
  
* **Cdd_Ipc_localEndPtId** – localEndPt need to be passed to retrieve the endpoint message object internally in the driver 

**Returns**: 

  
* **E_OK** 
<br> 
  
* **E_NOT_OK** 
<br> 

**Return**: Std_ReturnType

### function IpcNotifyUtils_lld_init

```
sint32 IpcNotifyUtils_lld_init(
    IpcNotifyUtils_Handle hIpcNotifyUtils
)
```

**Brief**: 
Initialize IPC Notify Utils module. 

This API will initialize the Utils module for IPC.

**Parameters**:

**Returns**: 

  
* **MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESS** if successful; else error on failure 
<br> 

**Return**: sint32 

### function IpcNotifyUtils_lld_sendSync

```
sint32 IpcNotifyUtils_lld_sendSync(
    IpcNotifyUtils_Handle hIpcNotifyUtils,
    uint32 remoteCoreId
)
```

**Brief**: 
Send a sync message to specific core. 

This API can be used to send sync message's to very specific core's For most users recommend to use the more simpler IpcNotify_syncAll() API.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **remoteCoreId** - Core ID, see CSL_CoreID for valid values. 

**Returns**: 

  
* **MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESSsync** message was sent successfully, else failure 
<br> 

**Return**: sint32 

### function IpcNotifyUtils_lld_waitSync

```
sint32 IpcNotifyUtils_lld_waitSync(
    IpcNotifyUtils_Handle hIpcNotifyUtils,
    uint32 remoteCoreId,
    uint32 timeout
)
```

**Brief**: 
Wait for a sync message to be received from the specified core. 

This API can be used to recevice sync message from very specific core's For most users recommend to use the more simpler IpcNotify_syncAll() API.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **remoteCoreId** - Core ID, see CSL_CoreID for valid values. 
  
* **timeout** - Amount of time in units of ticks to wait 

**Returns**: 

  
* **MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESSsync** message was received successfully 
<br> 
  
* **SystemP_TIMEOUTsync** message was NOT received after `timeout` ticks 
<br> 
  
* **SystemP_FAILUREinvalid** arguments 
<br> 

**Return**: sint32 

### function IpcNotifyUtils_lld_syncAll

```
sint32 IpcNotifyUtils_lld_syncAll(
    IpcNotifyUtils_Handle hIpcNotifyUtils,
    uint32 timeout
)
```

**Brief**: 
Send a message to all enabled cores and wait for sync message from all enabled cores. 

This API when called on all CPUs, make sure all CPUs execute upto a certain and then proceed only when all other CPUs have also executed to the same point.

This is useful esp during system init to make sure message exchange can be started only after all CPUs have finished their system initialization.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **timeout** - Amount of time in units of ticks to wait for the sync 

**Returns**: 

  
* **MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESSall** sync messages recevied successfully 
<br> 
  
* **SystemP_TIMEOUTsome** sync messages was NOT recevied after `timeout` ticks 
<br> 
  
* **SystemP_FAILUREinvalid** arguments 
<br> 

**Return**: sint32 

### function IpcNotifyUtils_lld_syncCallback

```
void IpcNotifyUtils_lld_syncCallback(
    void * hIpcNotify,
    uint32 remoteCoreId,
    uint16 localClientId,
    uint32 msgValue,
    uint32 timeout,
    sint32 crcStatus,
    void * args
)
```

void Cdd_Ipc_VringMemAllocation(
    void 
)
```

## Defines

| Name           |  Brief     |
| -------------- | ---------- |
| CDD_PWM_SW_MAJOR_VERSION   | Driver Implementation Major Version.  |
| CDD_PWM_SW_MINOR_VERSION   | Driver Implementation Minor Version.  |
| CDD_PWM_SW_PATCH_VERSION   | Driver Implementation Patch Version.  |
| CDD_PWM_AR_RELEASE_MAJOR_VERSION   | AUTOSAR Major version specification implemented by CDD_PWM Driver.  |
| CDD_PWM_AR_RELEASE_MINOR_VERSION   | AUTOSAR Minor version specification implemented by CDD_PWM Driver.  |
| CDD_PWM_AR_RELEASE_REVISION_VERSION   | AUTOSAR Patch version specification implemented by CDD_PWM Driver.  |
| CDD_PWM_VENDOR_ID   | Texas Instruments Vendor ID.  |
| CDD_PWM_MODULE_ID   | CDD_PWM Driver Module ID.  |
| CDD_PWM_INSTANCE_ID   | CDD_PWM Driver Instance ID.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_UNINIT   | API service used without module initialization.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_INIT_FAILED   | API Cdd_Pwm_Init service called with wrong parameter.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_PARAM_INVALID_CHANNEL   | API service used with an invalid channel Identifier.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_PARAM_INVALID_STATE   | API Cdd_Pwm_SetPinState() called with an invalid pin state or when the channel is in the RUNNING state..  |
| CDD_PWM_E_PARAM_INVALID_ACTION   | API Cdd_Pwm_SetPinAction() called with an invalid pin action.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_NO_VALID_NOTIF   | Usage of Cdd_Pwm_DisableNotification() or Cdd_Pwm_EnableNotification() on a channel where a NULL pointer is configured as the notification function.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_ALREADY_INITIALIZED   | API Cdd_Pwm_Init() called while the CDD_PWM driver has already been initialized.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_PARAM_POINTER   | API Cdd_Pwm_GetVersionInfo() is called with a NULL parameter.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_BUSY   | API Cdd_Pwm_StartChannel() called on a channel that is in state RUNNING.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_PARAM_NO_PIN   | Cdd_Pwm_SetPinState() or Cdd_Pwm_SetPinAction() called for a channel that doesn’t have an associated output pin.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_PARAM_INVALID_VALUE   | Cdd_Pwm_SetAbsoluteThreshold() or Cdd_Pwm_SetRelativeThreshold() called for with a compare match parameter greater than maximum supported counter value for a given channel.  |
| CDD_PWM_E_PARAM_CHANNEL   | Invalid Channel parameter  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_INIT   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Initialization API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_DEINIT   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for De-Initialization API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_STARTCHANNEL   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_StartChannel API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_STOPCHANNEL   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_StopChannel API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_GETCOUNTER_STATUS   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_SetPinState API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_SET_PIN_ACTION   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_SetPinAction API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_GETCOUNTER   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_GetCounter API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_SET_ABSOLUTE_THRESHOLD   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_SetAbsoluteThreshold API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_SET_RELATIVE_THRESHOLD   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_SetRelativeThreshold API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_DISABLE_NOTIFICATION   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_DisableNotification API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_ENABLE_NOTIFICATION   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_EnableNotification API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_GET_VERSION_INFO   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Cdd_Pwm_GetVersionInfo API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_REGISTER_READBACK   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for Critical register read back API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_SET_PERIOD_AND_DUTY   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for set duty cycle and period API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_SET_OUTPUT_TO_IDLE   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for get output state API.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_CONFIGURE_DEADBAND   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for configuration of DeadBand module.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_BYPASS_DEADBAND   | CDD_PWM driver service ID to bypass DeadBand module.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_CONFIGURE_CHOPPER   | CDD_PWM driver service ID for configuration of Chopper module.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_ENABLE_CHOPPER   | CDD_PWM driver service ID to enable Chopper module.  |
| CDD_PWM_SID_CLEAR_COUNTER_STATUS   | CDD_PWM driver service ID to Cdd_Pwm_ClearCounterStatus.  |

## Functions

| Return type    | Function Name           |      Brief        |
| -------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------- |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_Init](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_init)**(const Cdd_Pwm_ConfigType * ConfigPtr)| Function Initializes CDD_PWM unit.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_DeInit](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_deinit)**(void )| Function for PWM De-Initialization.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_StartChannelCounter](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_startchannelcounter)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType ChannelNumber)| Function to start a PWM channel.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_StopChannelCounter](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_stopchannelcounter)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType ChannelNumber)| Function to stop a PWM channel.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_SetPinAction](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_setpinaction)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType ChannelNumber, Cdd_Pwm_PinActionType PinAction)| Function to indicate the driver what shall be done automatically by hardware (if supported) upon compare match.  |
| Cdd_Pwm_ValueType | **[Cdd_Pwm_GetCounter](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_getcounter)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType ChannelNumber)| Function to read the current value of the counter.  |
| Cdd_Pwm_ReturnType | **[Cdd_Pwm_SetRelativeThreshold](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_setrelativethreshold)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType ChannelNumber, Cdd_Pwm_ValueType RelativeValue)| Function to set the value of the channel threshold relative to the current value of the counter.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_DisableNotification](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_disablenotification)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType ChannelNumber)| Function to disable the PWM signal edge notification.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_EnableNotification](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_enablenotification)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType ChannelParameter, Cdd_Pwm_InterruptType EdgeInterruptSource)| Function to enable the PWM signal edge notification according to notification parameter.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_GetVersionInfo](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_getversioninfo)**(Std_VersionInfoType * VersionInfoPtr)| Function to return the version information of this module.  |
| Std_ReturnType | **[Cdd_Pwm_RegisterReadback](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_registerreadback)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType Cdd_PwmChannel, Cdd_Pwm_RegisterReadbackType * RegRbPtr)| Function to read the important registers of the hardware unit and returns the value in the structure.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_SetPeriodDutyPhase](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_setperioddutyphase)**(Cdd_Pwm_channelParametertype ChannelParameter)| Function sets the period and the duty cycle of a PWM channel.  |
| void | **[Cdd_Pwm_SetOutputToIdle](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_setoutputtoidle)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType ChannelNumber)| Function sets the PWM output to the configured Idle state.  |
| Std_ReturnType | **[Cdd_Pwm_SyncEnable](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_syncenable)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType Channel, uint32 tbPhsValue, uint32 counterDir)| Function enables the synchronization of time base sub-module and also configures the phase count value to be loaded after sync event, counter direction after sync event.  |
| Std_ReturnType | **[Cdd_Pwm_SyncDisable](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_syncdisable)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType Channel)| Function disables the synchronization. Even if sync-in event occurs the count value will not be reloaded.  |
| uint16 | **[Cdd_Pwm_GetCounterStatus](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_getcounterstatus)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType Channel, uint32 tbStatusMask)| Function gets the Time Base status as indicated by the tbStatusMask parameter.  |
| Std_ReturnType | **[Cdd_Pwm_ClearCounterStatus](../Modules/group___c_d_d___p_w_m.md#function-cdd_pwm_clearcounterstatus)**(Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType Channel, uint32 tbStatusClrMask)| Function clears the Time base status bits indicated by the tbStatusClrMask parameter.  |

### function Cdd_Pwm_Init

```
void Cdd_Pwm_Init(
    const Cdd_Pwm_ConfigType * ConfigPtr
)
```

**Brief**: 
Function Initializes CDD_PWM unit. 

Cdd_Pwm_Init shall initialize all internals variables and the used PWM structure of the microcontroller according to the parameters specified in ConfigPtr.

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Non Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **ConfigPtr** - Pointer to driver configuration. 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None

### function Cdd_Pwm_CounterComparatorCfg

```
Std_ReturnType Cdd_Pwm_CounterComparatorCfg(
    Cdd_Pwm_ChannelType Channel,
    Cdd_Pwm_OutputChType cmpType,
    uint16 cmpVal,
    uint32 shadowToActiveLoadTrigger,
    uint32 overwriteShadow
)
```

**Brief**: 
Function configures the counter comparator and loads the comparator value. 

Cdd_Pwm_counterComparatorCfg configures the counter comparator and loads the comparator value. When Counter comparator value equals the counter value, then an event is generated both in the up direction and down direction.

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Non Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **Channel** 
  
* **cmpType** 
  
* **cmpVal** 
  
* **shadowToActiveLoadTrigger** 
  
* **overwriteShadow** 

**Returns**: 

  
* **E_OK** - Configuration of counter comparator is successful 
<br> 
  
* **E_NOT_OK** - Configuration of counter comparator is failed 
<br> 

**Return**: Std_ReturnType

title: Dio API GUIDE Header file

---

# DIO

## Types

|  Base type   | Type Name  | Brief |
| -------------| -----------| ----- |
|typedef uint32  | **[Dio_ChannelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_channeltype)** | type definition for identifying DIO channels (meaning single port pins)  | 
|typedef uint32  | **[Dio_PortType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_porttype)** | type definition for identifying ports  | 
|typedef uint8  | **[Dio_StatusType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_statustype)** | type definition for status  | 
|typedef uint32  | **[Dio_PortLevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_portleveltype)** | type definition for setting port level values  | 
|typedef uint8  | **[Dio_LevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_leveltype)** | type definition for setting DIO channnel level values (allowed values: STD_HIGH and STD_LOW)  | 
|typedef const [Dio_ChannelGroupType](../Classes/struct_dio___channel_group_type.md) *  | **[Dio_ChannelGroupRefType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_channelgroupreftype)** | type definition for referencing channel group structures  | 

### typedef Dio_ChannelType
```
typedef uint32 Dio_ChannelType;
```

**Brief**: 
type definition for identifying DIO channels (meaning single port pins) 

### typedef Dio_PortType
```
typedef uint32 Dio_PortType;
```

**Brief**: 
type definition for identifying ports 

### typedef Dio_StatusType
```
typedef uint8 Dio_StatusType;
```

**Brief**: 
type definition for status 

### typedef Dio_PortLevelType
```
typedef uint32 Dio_PortLevelType;
```

**Brief**: 
type definition for setting port level values 

This is a bitmask, where each bit with value '1' sets the corresponding port pin to HIGH and '0' sets the pin to LOW) 

### typedef Dio_LevelType
```
typedef uint8 Dio_LevelType;
```

**Brief**: 
type definition for setting DIO channnel level values (allowed values: STD_HIGH and STD_LOW) 

### typedef Dio_ChannelGroupRefType
```
typedef const Dio_ChannelGroupType* Dio_ChannelGroupRefType;
```

**Brief**: 
type definition for referencing channel group structures 

## Structures
|   Name         | Brief          |
| -------------- | -------------- |
| **[Dio_ChannelGroupType](../Classes/struct_dio___channel_group_type.md)** | Type definition for identifying channel groups (meaning a set of pins in one port)  |
| **[Dio_RegisterReadbackType](../Classes/struct_dio___register_readback_type.md)** | This type is used to access the GIO Registers.  |

## Defines

| Name           |  Brief     |
| -------------- | ---------- |
| DIO_SW_MAJOR_VERSION   | Driver Implementation Major Version.  |
| DIO_SW_MINOR_VERSION   | Driver Implementation Minor Version.  |
| DIO_SW_PATCH_VERSION   | Driver Implementation Patch Version.  |
| DIO_AR_RELEASE_MAJOR_VERSION   | AUTOSAR Major version specification implemented by DIO Driver.  |
| DIO_AR_RELEASE_MINOR_VERSION   | AUTOSAR Minor version specification implemented by DIO Driver.  |
| DIO_AR_RELEASE_REVISION_VERSION   | AUTOSAR Patch version specification implemented by DIO Driver.  |
| DIO_VENDOR_ID   | Texas Instruments Vendor ID.  |
| DIO_MODULE_ID   | DIO Driver Module ID.  |
| DIO_E_PARAM_INVALID_CHANNEL_ID   | ERROR:API service called with invalid channel ID.  |
| DIO_E_PARAM_INVALID_PORT_ID   | ERROR:API service called with invalid port ID.  |
| DIO_E_PARAM_INVALID_GROUP   | ERROR:API service called with invalid channel group ID.  |
| DIO_E_PARAM_POINTER   | ERROR:Dio_GetVersionInfo() called with NULL_PTR as parameter.  |
| DIO_SID_READ_CHANNEL   | Dio_ReadChannel()  |
| DIO_SID_WRITE_CHANNEL   | Dio_WriteChannel()  |
| DIO_SID_READ_PORT   | Dio_ReadPort()  |
| DIO_SID_WRITE_PORT   | Dio_WritePort()  |
| DIO_SID_READ_CHL_GROUP   | Dio_ReadChannelGroup()  |
| DIO_SID_WRITE_CHL_GROUP   | Dio_WriteChannelGroup()  |
| DIO_SID_FLIP_CHANNEL   | Dio_FlipChannel()  |
| DIO_SID_GET_VERSION_INFO   | Dio_GetVersionInfo()  |
| DIO_SID_REGISTER_READBACK   | Dio_RegisterReadback()  |
| DIO_RETURN_ZERO   | To Return 0 when Improper ID is passed to Read functions.  |

## Functions

| Return type    | Function Name           |      Brief        |
| -------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------- |
| [Dio_LevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_leveltype) | **[Dio_ReadChannel](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_readchannel)**([Dio_ChannelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_channeltype) ChannelId)| Returns the value of the specified DIO channel.  |
| void | **[Dio_WriteChannel](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_writechannel)**([Dio_ChannelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_channeltype) ChannelId, [Dio_LevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_leveltype) Level)| Service to set a level of a channel.  |
| [Dio_PortLevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_portleveltype) | **[Dio_ReadPort](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_readport)**([Dio_PortType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_porttype) PortId)| Returns the level of all channels of that port.  |
| void | **[Dio_WritePort](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_writeport)**([Dio_PortType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_porttype) PortId, [Dio_PortLevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_portleveltype) Level)| Service to set a value of the port.  |
| [Dio_PortLevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_portleveltype) | **[Dio_ReadChannelGroup](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_readchannelgroup)**([Dio_ChannelGroupRefType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_channelgroupreftype) ChannelGroupIdPtr)| This Service reads a subset of the adjoining bits of a port.  |
| void | **[Dio_WriteChannelGroup](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_writechannelgroup)**([Dio_ChannelGroupRefType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_channelgroupreftype) ChannelGroupIdPtr, [Dio_PortLevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_portleveltype) Level)| Service to set a subset of the adjoining bits of a port to a specified level.  |
| void | **[Dio_GetVersionInfo](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_getversioninfo)**(Std_VersionInfoType * versioninfo)| Service to get the version information of this module.  |
| [Dio_LevelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_leveltype) | **[Dio_FlipChannel](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_flipchannel)**([Dio_ChannelType](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#typedef-dio_channeltype) ChannelId)| Service to flip (change from 1 to 0 or from 0 to 1) the level of a channel and return the level of the channel after flip.  |
| Std_ReturnType | **[Dio_RegisterReadback](../Modules/group___d_i_o.md#function-dio_registerreadback)**([Dio_RegisterReadbackType](../Classes/struct_dio___register_readback_type.md) * RegRbPtr)| This function reads the important registers of the hardware unit and returns the value in the structure.  |

### function Dio_ReadChannel

```
Dio_LevelType Dio_ReadChannel(
    Dio_ChannelType ChannelId
)
```

**Brief**: 
Returns the value of the specified DIO channel. 

Service ID[hex] - 0x00

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **ChannelId** - ID of DIO channel 

**Returns**: 

  
* **STD_HIGH** - The physical level of the corresponding Pin is STD_HIGH 
<br> 
  
* **STD_LOW** - The physical level of the corresponding Pin is STD_LOW 
<br> 

**Return**: Dio_LevelType 

### function Dio_WriteChannel

```
void Dio_WriteChannel(
    Dio_ChannelType ChannelId,
    Dio_LevelType Level
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service to set a level of a channel. 

Service ID[hex] - 0x01

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **ChannelId** - ID of DIO channel 
  
* **Level** - Value to be written 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None 

### function Dio_ReadPort

```
Dio_PortLevelType Dio_ReadPort(
    Dio_PortType PortId
)
```

**Brief**: 
Returns the level of all channels of that port. 

Service ID[hex] - 0x02

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **PortId** - ID of DIO Port 

**Returns**: 

  
* **Level** of all channels of that port 
<br> 

**Return**: Dio_PortLevelType 

### function Dio_WritePort

```
void Dio_WritePort(
    Dio_PortType PortId,
    Dio_PortLevelType Level
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service to set a value of the port. 

Service ID[hex] - 0x03

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **PortId** - ID of DIO Port 
  
* **Level** - Value to be written 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None 

### function Dio_ReadChannelGroup

```
Dio_PortLevelType Dio_ReadChannelGroup(
    Dio_ChannelGroupRefType ChannelGroupIdPtr
)
```

**Brief**: 
This Service reads a subset of the adjoining bits of a port. 

Service ID[hex] - 0x04

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **ChannelGroupIdPtr** - Pointer to ChannelGroup 

**Returns**: 

  
* **Level** of a subset of the adjoining bits of a port 
<br> 

**Return**: Dio_PortLevelType 

### function Dio_WriteChannelGroup

```
void Dio_WriteChannelGroup(
    Dio_ChannelGroupRefType ChannelGroupIdPtr,
    Dio_PortLevelType Level
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service to set a subset of the adjoining bits of a port to a specified level. 

Service ID[hex] - 0x05

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **ChannelGroupIdPtr** - Pointer to ChannelGroup 
  
* **Level** - Value to be written 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None 

### function Dio_GetVersionInfo

```
void Dio_GetVersionInfo(
    Std_VersionInfoType * versioninfo
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service to get the version information of this module. 

Service ID[hex] - 0x12

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **versioninfo** - Pointer to where to store the version information of this module. 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None 

### function Dio_FlipChannel

```
Dio_LevelType Dio_FlipChannel(
    Dio_ChannelType ChannelId
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service to flip (change from 1 to 0 or from 0 to 1) the level of a channel and return the level of the channel after flip. 

Service ID[hex] - 0x11

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **ChannelId** - ID of DIO channel 

**Returns**: 

  
* **STD_HIGH** - The physical level of the corresponding Pin is STD_HIGH 
<br> 
  
* **STD_LOW** - The physical level of the corresponding Pin is STD_LOW 
<br> 

**Return**: Dio_LevelType 

### function Dio_RegisterReadback

```
Std_ReturnType Dio_RegisterReadback(
    Dio_RegisterReadbackType * RegRbPtr
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function reads the important registers of the hardware unit and returns the value in the structure. 

This API is used to check the status of critical registers which do not change during program execution.

Service ID[hex] - 0x19

Sync/Async - Synchronous

Reentrancy - Reentrant

This function reads the important registers of the hardware unit and returns the value in the structure. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **RegRbPtr** - Pointer to where to store the readback values. If this pointer is NULL, then the API will return E_NOT_OK. 

**Returns**: 

  
* **E_OK** - Register read back has been done successfully 
<br> 
  
* **E_NOT_OK** - Register read back failed
<br> 

**Return**: Std_ReturnType

# DMA

## Types

|  Base type   | Type Name  | Brief |
| -------------| -----------| ----- |
|enum| **[CddDma_StatusType](../Modules/group___d_m_a.md#enum-cdddma_statustype)** | This type defines a range of specific status for Complex driver.  | 
|typedef [Cdd_Edma_EventCallback](../Modules/group___d_r_v___c_d_d___e_d_m_a___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-cdd_edma_eventcallback)  | **[CddDma_CallBackType](../Modules/group___d_m_a.md#typedef-cdddma_callbacktype)** | Callback type.  | 

### enum CddDma_StatusType
| Enumerator | Value | Description |
| ---------- | ----- | ----------- |
| CDD_DMA_UNINIT |       0U| The Complex Driver is not initialized or not usable.   |
| CDD_DMA_IDLE |         1U| The Complex Driver is not currently transferring.   |
| CDD_DMA_ALLOW_ACCESS | 2U| The Complex Driver is currently open.   |
| CDD_DMA_BUSY |         3U| The Complex Driver is performing a write operation.   |
| CDD_DMA_CLOSE |        4U| The Complex Driver is currently closed.   |

**Brief**: 
This type defines a range of specific status for Complex driver. 

### typedef CddDma_CallBackType
```
typedef Cdd_Edma_EventCallback CddDma_CallBackType;
```

**Brief**: 
Callback type.

### function Cdd_Dma_CbkRegister

```
uint32 Cdd_Dma_CbkRegister(
    uint32 handleId,
    void * appdata,
    Cdd_Edma_EventCallback callback
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service for CDD_DMA Callback Register. Function to register callback function for a TCC. 

Service ID[hex] : 0x03

Sync/Async : Synchronous

Reentrancy : Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **handleId** - Cdd_Dma handle is passed for which we want to register callback 
  
* **appdata** - data associated with it 
  
* **callback** - registered callback which will be called on interrupt 

**Returns**: 

  
* **E_OK** Status Ok 
<br> 
  
* **E_NOT_OK** Status Not Ok 
<br> 

**Return**: uint32 

### function Cdd_Dma_ParamSet

```
void Cdd_Dma_ParamSet(
    uint32 handleId,
    uint32 channelIdx,
    uint32 paramIndex,
    Cdd_Dma_ParamEntry paramEntry
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service for CDD_DMA Param Setting. Function to Set the paramSet values with the param value associated with the channel of a particular handle. 

Service ID[hex] : 0x04

Sync/Async : Synchronous

Reentrancy : Non-Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **handleId** - Cdd_Dma handle is passed for which we want to set the param set value 
  
* **channelIdx** - Index of the channel for that particular handle 
  
* **paramIndex** - Index of the param for which we want to set the param values 
  
* **paramEntry** - Structure which contains the required paramSet fields to be used for the particular param value. 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None 

### function Cdd_Dma_LinkChannel

void Cdd_Dma_LinkChannel(
    uint32 handleId,
    uint32 paramIndex0,
    uint32 paramIndex1
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service for CDD_DMA Linking params. Function to Link the param. Often used to link different params within the channel. 

Service ID[hex] : 0x05

Sync/Async : Synchronous

Reentrancy : Non-Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **handleId** - Cdd_Dma handle is passed for which we want to used linking 
  
* **paramIndex0** - Index of the param which should be linked to another param 
  
* **paramIndex1** - Index of the param which is supposed to be linked to the first one 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None 

### function Cdd_Dma_ChainChannel

```
void Cdd_Dma_ChainChannel(
    uint32 handleId,
    uint32 channelIdx0,
    uint32 paramIndex0,
    uint32 channelIdx1,
    uint32 opt
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service for CDD_DMA Chaining Channels Function to Chain multiple channel and can be used in transmission only with one trigger. 

Service ID[hex] : 0x06

Sync/Async : Synchronous

Reentrancy : Non-Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **handleId** - Cdd_Dma handle is passed for which we want to used Chaining 
  
* **channelIdx0** - Index of the first channel which is used for Chaining 
  
* **paramIndex0** - Index of the param which is supposed to be linked to the first one 
  
* **channelIdx1** - Index of the second channel which is used for Chaining 
  
* **opt** - Chain Option to be used while Chaining 

**Returns**: 

  
* **None** 
<br> 

**Return**: None

Service ID[hex] : 0x07

Reentrancy : Non-Reentrant

* **handleId** - Cdd_Dma handle is passed for which we want to enable the transfer

### function Cdd_Dma_DisableTransferRegion

```
boolean Cdd_Dma_DisableTransferRegion(
    uint32 handleId,
    uint32 trigMode
)
```

**Brief**: 
Service for CDD_DMA Disable transfer region. Function to Disable the trigger for transmission. 

Service ID[hex] : 0x0C

Sync/Async : Synchronous

Reentrancy : Non-Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **handleId** - Cdd_Dma handle is passed for which we want to disable the transfer 
  
* **trigMode** - Trigger mode which is used to disable the transmission 

**Returns**: 

  
* **Indicate** successfully disabled transfer or not with value as True or False 
<br> 

**Return**: boolean 

### function Cdd_Dma_RegisterReadback

```
Std_ReturnType Cdd_Dma_RegisterReadback(
    uint32 handleId,
    Cdd_Dma_RegisterReadbackType * RegPtr
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function reads the important registers of the hardware unit and returns the value in the structure. 

Service ID[hex] : 0x0D

Sync/Async : Synchronous

Reentrancy : Non-Reentrant

**Parameters**: 

  
* **handleId** - Cdd_Dma handle is passed for which we want to read the register value 
  
* **RegPtr** - Pointer to where to store the readback values. If this pointer is NULL_PTR, then the API will return E_NOT_OK. 

**Returns**: 

  
* **E_OK** Register read back has been done 
<br> 
  
* **E_NOT_OK** Register read back failed 
<br> 

**Return**: Std_ReturnType

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_ccParamEntry_Init

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_ccParamEntry_Init(
    CDD_EDMACCEDMACCPaRAMEntry * paramEntry
)
```

**Brief**: 
EDMA interrupt configuration object. The object is passed to the CDD_EDMA_registerIntr() function. This is held by the driver till the CDD_EDMA_unregisterIntr() function is called with this obect. The application should not modify this object in between. 

Clear a PaRAM Set .

**Parameters**: 

  
* **paramEntry** Parameter RAM set to be cleared. 

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_enableChInShadowRegRegion

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_enableChInShadowRegRegion(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 regionId,
    uint32 chNum
)
```

**Brief**: 
Enable channel to Shadow region mapping. 

This API allocates DMA channels or TCCs, and the same resources are enabled in the shadow region specific register (DRAE/DRAEH). Here only one shadow region is used since, there is only one Master.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **regionId** Region id to be used. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_REGIONS-1 
  
* **chNum** Allocated channel number. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA 

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_disableChInShadowRegRegion

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_disableChInShadowRegRegion(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 regionId,
    uint32 chNum
)
```

**Brief**: 
Disable channel to Shadow region mapping. 

This API deallocates DMA channels or TCCs, and the same resources are disabled in the shadow region specific register (DRAE/DRAEH). Here only one shadow region is used since, there is only one Master.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **regionId** Region id to be used. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_REGIONS-1 
  
* **chNum** Allocated channel number. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA 

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_channelToParamMap

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_channelToParamMap(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 channel,
    uint32 paramSet
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function maps DMA channel to any of the PaRAM sets in the PaRAM memory map. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **channel** The DMA channel number required to be mapped.
  
* **paramSet** It specifies the paramSet to which DMA channel required to be mapped. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_PARAMSETS-1 

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_mapChToEvtQ

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_mapChToEvtQ(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 chNum,
    uint32 evtQNum
)
```

**Brief**: 
Map channel to Event Queue. 

This API maps DMA channels to the Event Queue

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **chNum** Allocated channel number. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA 
  
* **evtQNum** Event Queue Number to which the channel will be mapped (valid only for the Master Channel (DMA) request).

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_unmapChToEvtQ

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_unmapChToEvtQ(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 chNum
)
```

**Brief**: 
Remove Mapping of channel to Event Queue. 

This API Unmaps DMA channels from the Event Queue allocated

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **chNum** Allocated channel number. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA

 Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA 

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_clrCCErr

```

    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 flags
)
```

CDD_EDMACC_CLR_TCCERR Clears the TCCERR bit in the EDMACC ERR Reg

CDD_EDMACC_CLR_QTHRQ0 Queue threshold error clear for queue 0.

CDD_EDMACC_CLR_QTHRQ1 Queue threshold error clear for queue 1. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **flags** Masks to be passed.

 flags can have values:

Note : This API is generally used during Manual transfers.

    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 regionId,
    uint32 chNum
)
```

**Brief**: 
Enables the user to Disable an DMA event. 

Note : Writes of 1 to the bits in EECR clear the corresponding event bits in EER; writes of 0 have no effect.. This is generally used for Event Based transfers.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **regionId** Region id to be used. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_REGIONS-1

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_enableEvtIntrRegion

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_enableEvtIntrRegion(

)
```

**Brief**: 
Enables the user to enable the transfer completion interrupt generation by the EDMACC for all DMA/QDMA channels. 

Note : To set any interrupt bit in IER, a 1 must be written to the corresponding interrupt bit in the interrupt enable set register. 

**Parameters**:

* **regionId** Region id to be used. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_REGIONS-1

Note : Writes of 1 to the bits in IECR clear the corresponding interrupt bits in the interrupt enable registers (IER); writes of 0 have no effect.

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_getPaRAM

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_getPaRAM(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 paRAMId,
    CDD_EDMACCEDMACCPaRAMEntry * currPaRAM
)
```

**Brief**: 
Retrieve existing PaRAM set associated with specified logical channel (DMA/Link). 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **paRAMId** paRAMset ID whose parameter set is requested. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_PARAMSETS-1
  
* **currPaRAM** User gets the existing PaRAM here.

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_setPaRAM

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_setPaRAM(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 paRAMId,
    const CDD_EDMACCEDMACCPaRAMEntry * newPaRAM
)
```

**Brief**: 
Copy the user specified PaRAM Set onto the PaRAM Set associated with the logical channel (DMA/Link). 

This API takes a PaRAM Set as input and copies it onto the actual PaRAM Set associated with the logical channel. OPT field of the PaRAM Set is written first and the CCNT field is written last.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **paRAMId** paRAMset ID whose parameter set has to be updated Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_PARAMSETS-1
  
* **newPaRAM** Parameter RAM set to be copied onto existing PaRAM.

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_configureChannelRegion

```
uint32 CDD_EDMA_lld_configureChannelRegion(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 regionId,
    const Cdd_Dma_ChannelConfigType * chConfig
)
```

**Brief**: 
Request a DMA channel. 

Each channel (DMA) must be requested before initiating a DMA transfer on that channel.

This API is used to allocate a logical channel (DMA) along with the associated resources. For DMA channels, TCC and PaRAM Set are also allocated along with the requested channel.

User can request a specific logical channel by passing the channel number in 'chConfig->chNum'.

For DMA channels, after allocating all the EDMA resources, this API sets the TCC field of the OPT PaRAM Word with the allocated TCC. It also sets the event queue for the channel allocated. The event queue needs to be specified by the user.

For DMA channel, it also sets the DCHMAP register.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **regionId** Region id to be used. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_REGIONS-1 
  
* **chConfig** Pointer to channel configuration structure containing the following fields:

* chNum: This is the channel number requested for a particular event. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA 
* tccNum: The TCC number on which the completion/error interrupt is generated. Not used if user requested for a Link channel.
* paramId: The param number corresponding to the DMA channel.
* evtQNum: Event Queue Number to which the channel will be mapped (valid only for the Master Channel (DMA) request).

**Return**: TRUE if parameters are valid, else FALSE 

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_freeChannelRegion

```
uint32 CDD_EDMA_lld_freeChannelRegion(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 regionId,
    uint32 chNum,
    uint32 trigMode
)
```

**Brief**: 
Free the specified channel (DMA/Link) and its associated resources (PaRAM Set, TCC etc) and removes various mappings. 

For Link channels, this API only frees the associated PaRAM Set.

For DMA channels, it does the following operations: 1) Disable any ongoing transfer on the channel,

2) Remove the channel to Event Queue mapping,

3) For DMA channels, clear the DCHMAP register, if available

4) Frees the DMA channel in the end.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **regionId** Region id to be used. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_REGIONS-1 
  
* **chNum** This is the channel number requested for a particular event. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA 
  
* **trigMode** Mode of triggering start of transfer.

 trigMode can have values: CDD_EDMA_TRIG_MODE_MANUAL

 CDD_EDMA_TRIG_MODE_EVENT

**Return**: TRUE if parameters are valid else return FALSE 

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_enableTransferRegion

```
uint32 CDD_EDMA_lld_enableTransferRegion(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 regionId,
    uint32 chNum,
    uint32 trigMode
)
```

**Brief**: 
Start EDMA transfer on the specified channel. 

There are multiple ways to trigger an EDMA transfer. The triggering mode option allows choosing from the available triggering modes: Event, Manual or QDMA.

In event triggered, a peripheral or an externally generated event triggers the transfer. This API clears the Event and Event Miss Register and then enables the DMA channel by writing to the EESR.

In manual triggered mode, CPU manually triggers a transfer by writing a 1 in the Event Set Register ESR. This API writes to the ESR to start the transfer.

In QDMA triggered mode, a QDMA transfer is triggered when a CPU (or other EDMA programmer) writes to the trigger word of the QDMA channel PaRAM set (auto-triggered) or when the EDMACC performs a link update on a PaRAM set that has been mapped to a QDMA channel (link triggered). This API enables the QDMA channel by writing to the QEESR register.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **regionId** Region id to be used. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_REGIONS-1 
  
* **chNum** Channel being used to enable transfer. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA
  
* **trigMode** Mode of triggering start of transfer (Manual, QDMA or Event).

 trigMode can have values: CDD_EDMA_TRIG_MODE_MANUAL

 CDD_EDMA_TRIG_MODE_QDMA

 CDD_EDMA_TRIG_MODE_EVENT

**Return**: retVal TRUE or FALSE depending on the param passed.

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_disableTransferRegion

```
uint32 CDD_EDMA_lld_disableTransferRegion(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 regionId,
    uint32 chNum,
    uint32 trigMode
)
```

**Brief**: 
Disable DMA transfer on the specified channel. 

There are multiple ways by which an EDMA transfer could be triggered. The triggering mode option allows choosing from the available triggering modes.

To disable a channel which was previously triggered in manual mode, this API clears the Secondary Event Register and Event Miss Register, if set, for the specific DMA channel.

To disable a channel which was previously triggered in QDMA mode, this API clears the QDMA Event Enable Register, for the specific QDMA channel.

To disable a channel which was previously triggered in event mode, this API clears the Event Enable Register, Event Register, Secondary Event Register and Event Miss Register, if set, for the specific DMA channel.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **regionId** Region id to be used. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_REGIONS-1 
  
* **chNum** Channel being used to enable transfer. Valid values are 0 to CDD_EDMA_NUM_DMACH-1 for DMA
  
* **trigMode** Mode of triggering start of transfer (Manual, QDMA or Event).

**Return**: retVal TRUE or FALSE depending on the param passed.

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_intrStatusHighGetRegion

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_chainChannel

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_chainChannel(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 paRAMId1,
    uint32 chId2,
    uint32 chainOptions
)
```

**Brief**: 
Chain the two specified channels. 

This API is used to chain a DMA channel to a previously allocated DMA/QDMA channel

Chaining is different from Linking. The EDMA link feature reloads the current channel parameter set with the linked parameter set. The EDMA chaining feature does not modify or update any channel parameter set; it provides a synchronization event (or trigger) to the chained DMA channel, as soon as the transfer (final or intermediate) completes on the main DMA/QDMA channel.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **paRAMId1** PaRAM set ID of physical channel1 to which particular paRAM set will be chained or PaRAM set ID in case another PaRAM set is being chained to this PaRAM set
  
* **chId2** DMA channel which needs to be chained to the first DMA/QDMA channel.
  
* **chainOptions** combination of the following masks which control individual PaRAM OPT fields related to intermediate/final completion chaining and intermediate/final completion interrupt :

* CDD_EDMA_OPT_TCCHEN_MASK
* CDD_EDMA_OPT_ITCCHEN_MASK
* CDD_EDMA_OPT_TCINTEN_MASK
* CDD_EDMA_OPT_ITCINTEN_MASK e.g to enable final completion chaining and enable interrupt only for intermediate completion, set chainOptions as : CDD_EDMA_OPT_TCCHEN_MASK | CDD_EDMA_OPT_ITCINTEN_MASK 

### function CDD_EDMA_lld_linkChannel

```
void CDD_EDMA_lld_linkChannel(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 paRAMId1,
    uint32 paRAMId2
)
```

**Brief**: 
Link two channels. 

This API is used to link two previously allocated logical (DMA/QDMA/Link) channels.

It sets the Link field of the PaRAM set associated with first channel (chId1) to point it to the PaRAM set associated with second channel (chId2).

It also sets the TCC field of PaRAM set of second channel to the same as that of the first channel.

After the transfer based on the PaRAM set of channel1 is over, the PaRAM set paRAMId2 will be copied to the PaRAM set of channel1 and transfer will resume. For DMA channels, another sync event is required to initiate the transfer on the Link channel. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** Memory address of the EDMA instance used.
  
* **paRAMId1** PaRAM set ID of physical channel1 to which particular paRAM set will be linked or PaRAM set ID in case another PaRAM set is being linked to this PaRAM set
  
* **paRAMId2** PaRAM set ID which is linked to channel with parameter ID paRAMId1

## Detailed Description

This module contains APIs to program and use the EDMA.

title: APIs for ECAP

# DRV_ECAP_MODULE

 [More...](#detailed-description)

## Files

| Name           |
| -------------- |
| **[V0/ecap.h](../Files/ecap_8h.md#file-ecap.h)** <br>This file contains the function prototypes for the device abstraction layer for ECAP. It also contains some related macro definitions and some files to be included.  |

## Types

|  Base type   | Type Name  | Brief |
| -------------| -----------| ----- |
|enum| **[ECAP_EmulationMode](../Modules/group___d_r_v___e_c_a_p___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#enum-ecap_emulationmode)** 
|enum| **[ECAP_SyncInPulseSource](../Modules/group___d_r_v___e_c_a_p___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#enum-ecap_syncinpulsesource)** 
|enum| **[ECAP_QualPeriodSelect](../Modules/group___d_r_v___e_c_a_p___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#enum-ecap_qualperiodselect)** 

### enum ECAP_EmulationMode
| Enumerator | Value | Description |
| ---------- | ----- | ----------- |
| ECAP_EMULATION_STOP | 0x0U| TSCTR is stopped on emulation suspension.   |
| ECAP_EMULATION_RUN_TO_ZERO | 0x1U| TSCTR runs until 0 before stopping on emulation suspension.   |
| ECAP_EMULATION_FREE_RUN | 0x2U| TSCTR is not affected by emulation suspension.   |

### enum ECAP_SyncInPulseSource
| Enumerator | Value | Description |
| ---------- | ----- | ----------- |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_DISABLE | 0x0| Disable Sync-in.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM0 | 0x1| Sync-in source is EPWM0 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM1 | 0x2| Sync-in source is EPWM1 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM2 | 0x3| Sync-in source is EPWM2 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM3 | 0x4| Sync-in source is EPWM3 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM4 | 0x5| Sync-in source is EPWM4 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM5 | 0x6| Sync-in source is EPWM5 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM6 | 0x7| Sync-in source is EPWM6 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM7 | 0x8| Sync-in source is EPWM7 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM8 | 0x9| Sync-in source is EPWM8 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM9 | 0xA| Sync-in source is EPWM9 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM10 | 0xB| Sync-in source is EPWM10 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM11 | 0xC| Sync-in source is EPWM11 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM12 | 0xD| Sync-in source is EPWM12 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM13 | 0xE| Sync-in source is EPWM13 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM14 | 0xF| Sync-in source is EPWM14 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM15 | 0x10| Sync-in source is EPWM15 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM16 | 0x11| Sync-in source is EPWM16 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM17 | 0x12| Sync-in source is EPWM17 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM18 | 0x13| Sync-in source is EPWM18 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM19 | 0x14| Sync-in source is EPWM19 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM20 | 0x15| Sync-in source is EPWM20 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM21 | 0x16| Sync-in source is EPWM21 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM22 | 0x17| Sync-in source is EPWM22 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM23 | 0x18| Sync-in source is EPWM23 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM24 | 0x19| Sync-in source is EPWM24 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM25 | 0x1A| Sync-in source is EPWM25 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM26 | 0x1B| Sync-in source is EPWM26 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM27 | 0x1C| Sync-in source is EPWM27 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM28 | 0x1D| Sync-in source is EPWM28 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM29 | 0x1E| Sync-in source is EPWM29 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM30 | 0x1F| Sync-in source is EPWM30 sync-out signal.   |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_PULSE_SRC_SYNCOUT_EPWM31 | 0x20| Sync-in source is EPWM31 sync-out signal.   |

### enum ECAP_QualPeriodSelect
| Enumerator | Value | Description |
| ---------- | ----- | ----------- |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_BYPASS | 0x0| Bypass.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE1 | 0x1| Pulse width less than 1 cycle.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE2 | 0x2| Pulse width less than 2 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE3 | 0x3| Pulse width less than 3 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE4 | 0x4| Pulse width less than 4 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE5 | 0x5| Pulse width less than 5 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE6 | 0x6| Pulse width less than 6 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE7 | 0x7| Pulse width less than 7 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE8 | 0x8| Pulse width less than 8 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE9 | 0x9| Pulse width less than 9 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE10 | 0xA| Pulse width less than 10 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE11 | 0xB| Pulse width less than 11 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE12 | 0xC| Pulse width less than 12 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE13 | 0xD| Pulse width less than 13 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE14 | 0xE| Pulse width less than 14 cycles.   |
| ECAP_PULSE_WIDTH_FILTER_CYCLE15 | 0xF| Pulse width less than 15 cycles.   |

Values that can be passed to ECAP_selectQualPeriod() as the _mode_ parameter. 

## Defines

| Name           |  Brief     |
| -------------- | ---------- |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_MODE   | Capture Mode.  |
| ECAP_APWM_MODE   | Auxiliary PWM Mode.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT_1   | Time stamp capture on event 1.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT_2   | Time stamp capture on event 2.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT_3   | Time stamp capture on event 3.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT_4   | Time stamp capture on event 4.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT1_STOP   | Stop capture after event 1.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT2_STOP   | Stop capture after event 2.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT3_STOP   | Stop capture after event 3.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT4_STOP   | Stop capture after event 4.  |
| ECAP_APWM_ACTIVE_HIGH   | Output Polarity HIGH.  |
| ECAP_APWM_ACTIVE_LOW   | Output Polarity LOW.  |
| ECAP_COUNTER_STOP   | Counter Mode Stop.  |
| ECAP_COUNTER_FREE_RUNNING   | Counter Mode Free Running.  |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN_DISABLE   | Disable SyncIn option.  |
| ECAP_ENABLE_COUNTER   | Enable Counter to be loaded from the ECAP_CNTPHS register upon SYNCI signal.  |
| ECAP_SYNC_IN   | Select SyncIn event to be the SyncOut signal.  |
| ECAP_PRD_EQ   | Select PRD event to be the SyncOut signal.  |
| ECAP_SYNC_OUT_DISABLE   | Disable SyncOut Signal.  |
| ECAP_CEVT1_INT   | Capture Event 1 Interrupt Enable.  |
| ECAP_CEVT2_INT   | Capture Event 2 Interrupt Enable.  |
| ECAP_CEVT3_INT   | Capture Event 3 Interrupt Enable.  |
| ECAP_CEVT4_INT   | Capture Event 4 Interrupt Enable.  |
| ECAP_CNTOVF_INT   | Counter Overflow Interrupt Enable.  |
| ECAP_PRDEQ_INT   | Period Equal Interrupt Enable.  |
| ECAP_CMPEQ_INT   | Compare Equal Interrupt Enable.  |
| ECAP_GLOBAL_INT   | Global Interrupt Source Clear Flag.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT_RISING   | Capture Event Rising Edge.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT_FALLING   | Capture Event Falling Edge.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT_RESET_COUNTER_NO_RESET   | No counter reset upon Capture Event.  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_EVENT_RESET_COUNTER_RESET   | Counter reset upon Capture Event.  |
| ICU_START_SEC_CODE   |  |
| ICU_STOP_SEC_CODE   |  |
| ECAP_CAPTURE_INPUT_SOURCE_SELECT_0   | Capture input source select input is ECAPxINPU[0].  |
| CSL_ECAP_ECCLR_INT_MASK   |  |
| CSL_ECAP_ECCLR   |  |
| CSL_ECAP_ECCTL2   |  |
| CSL_ECAP_ECCTL2_REARM_MASK   |  |

### function ECAP_reArm

```
static inline void ECAP_reArm(
    uint32 base
)
```

**Brief**: 
Write to RE-ARM bit. 

### function ECAP_clearGlobalInterrupt

```
static inline void ECAP_clearGlobalInterrupt(
    uint32 base
)
```

**Brief**: 
Write to INT bit. 

### function ICU_ECAP_captureLoadingEnable

```
void ICU_ECAP_captureLoadingEnable(
    uint32 baseAddr
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function enables capture loading. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 

### function ICU_ECAP_captureLoadingDisable

```
void ICU_ECAP_captureLoadingDisable(
    uint32 baseAddr
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function disables capture loading. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used.

)

### function ICU_ECAP_prescaleConfig

```
void ICU_ECAP_prescaleConfig(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 prescale
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function configures prescale value. 

Prescale can take any integer value between 0 to 31 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 
  
* **prescale** It is the value which is used to prescale the incoming input.

### function ICU_ECAP_operatingModeSelect

```
void ICU_ECAP_operatingModeSelect(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 modeSelect
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function configures ecapture module to operate in capture mode or in APWM mode. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 
  
* **modeSelect** It is the value which determines whether ecapture module to operate in capture mode or in APWM mode.

 modeSelect can take one of the following macros.

* [Ecap_OperMode_t]. 

### function ICU_ECAP_timeStampRead

```
uint32 ICU_ECAP_timeStampRead(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 capEvtFlag
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function returns time-stamp for a given capture event. 

capEvtFlag can take one of the following macros.

* [Ecap_CaptEvt_t].

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 
  
* **capEvtFlag** It is the value which determines for which capture event time-stam has to returned.

**Return**: Returns the time-stamp for given capure event. 

### function ICU_ECAP_captureEvtPolarityConfig

```
void ICU_ECAP_captureEvtPolarityConfig(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 capEvt1pol,
    uint32 capEvt2pol,
    uint32 capEvt3pol,
    uint32 capEvt4pol
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function configures Capture Event polarity. 

capEvtpol variables can take one of the following macros.

* [Ecap_CaptureEvtPolarityConfig_t]. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 
  
* **capEvt1pol** It determines whether Capture Event1 has to be generated on rising edge or falling edge of pulse.
  
* **capEvt2pol** It determines whether Capture Event2 has to be generated on rising edge or falling edge of pulse.
  
* **capEvt3pol** It determines whether Capture Event3 has to be generated on rising edge or falling edge of pulse.
  
* **capEvt4pol** It determines whether Capture Event4 has to be generated on rising edge or falling edge of pulse.

### function ICU_ECAP_captureEvtCntrRstConfig

```
void ICU_ECAP_captureEvtCntrRstConfig(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 counterRst1,
    uint32 counterRst2,
    uint32 counterRst3,
    uint32 counterRst4
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function enables reset of the counters upon Capture Events. 

counterRst variables can take one of the following macros.

* [Ecap_CaptureEvtCntrRstConfig_t]. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 
  
* **counterRst1** It determines whether counter has to be reset upon Capture Event1.
  
* **counterRst2** It determines whether counter has to be reset upon Capture Event2.
  
* **counterRst3** It determines whether counter has to be reset upon Capture Event3.
  
* **counterRst4** It determines whether counter has to be reset upon Capture Event4.

### function ICU_ECAP_continousModeConfig

```
void ICU_ECAP_continousModeConfig(
    uint32 baseAddr
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function configures ECAP to Continuous mode. 

This API is valid only if ECAP is configured to Capture Mode.It has no significance when ECAP is configured in APWM mode.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 

### function ICU_ECAP_counterControl

```
void ICU_ECAP_counterControl(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 flag
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function configures counter to stop or free running based on its input argument flag. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 
  
* **flag** It is the value which determine counter to be configured to stop or free running.

 flag can take one of the following macros.

* [Ecap_CounterMode_t]. 

### function ICU_ECAP_syncInOutSelect

```
void ICU_ECAP_syncInOutSelect(
    uint32 baseAddr,
    uint32 syncIn,
    uint32 syncOut
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function configures Sync-In and Sync-Out. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used. 
  
* **syncIn** It is the value which determines whether to disable syncIn or to enable counter to be loaded from CNTPHS register upon a SYNCI signal.

 syncIn can take one of the following macros.

* [Ecap_CounterSyncInMode_t].
  
* **syncOut** It is the value which select type of syncOut signal (i.e select syncIn event to be the Sync-Out signal, select PRD_eq event to be Sync-Out signal).

 syncOut can take one of the following macros.

* [Ecap_CounterSyncInMode_t]. 

### function ICU_ECAP_globalIntrClear

void ICU_ECAP_globalIntrClear(
    uint32 baseAddr
)
```

**Brief**: 
This function clears global interrupt and enables the generation of interrupts if any of the event interrupt are enabled and corresponding event interrupt flag is set. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **baseAddr** It is the Memory address of the ECAP instance used.

## Detailed Description

This module contains APIs to program and use the ECAP module. 



---
title: APIs for IPC Notify LLD

---

# DRV_IPC_NOTIFY_LLD_MODULE

## Types

|  Base type   | Type Name  | Brief |
| -------------| -----------| ----- |
|typedef void(*)(void *hIpcNotify, uint32 remoteCoreId, uint16 localClientId, uint32 msgValue, uint32 timeout, sint32 crcStatus, void *args)  | **[IpcNotify_FxnCallback](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___n_o_t_i_f_y___l_l_d___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-ipcnotify_fxncallback)** 
|typedef sint32(*)(uint8 *data, uint16 dataLen, uint8 crcSize, void *crc)  | **[IpcCrcHookFxn](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___n_o_t_i_f_y___l_l_d___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-ipccrchookfxn)** 
|typedef [IpcNotify_InitObject](../Classes/struct_ipc_notify___init_object.md) *  | **[IpcNotify_InitHandle](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___n_o_t_i_f_y___l_l_d___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-ipcnotify_inithandle)** 
|typedef [IpcNotify_Object](../Classes/struct_ipc_notify___object.md) *  | **[IpcNotify_Handle](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___n_o_t_i_f_y___l_l_d___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-ipcnotify_handle)** 

### typedef IpcNotify_FxnCallback

### function IpcNotify_lld_init

```
sint32 IpcNotify_lld_init(
    IpcNotify_Handle hIpcNotify
)
```

**Brief**: 
Initialize IPC Notify module. 

This API will initialize the HW used for IPC.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **hIpcNotify** IPC Notify LLD handle

**Return**: MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESS if successful; else error on failure 

### function IpcNotify_lld_deInit

```
sint32 IpcNotify_lld_deInit(
    IpcNotify_Handle hIpcNotify
)
```

**Brief**: 
De-initialize IPC Notify module. 

This API will de-initialize the HW used for IPC.

**Parameters**: 

  
* **hIpcNotify** IPC Notify LLD handle

**Return**: MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESS if successful; else error on failure 

### function IpcNotify_lld_sendMsg

```
sint32 IpcNotify_lld_sendMsg(
    IpcNotify_Handle hIpcNotify,
    const IpcNotify_MsgParams * msgParams
)
```

**Brief**: 
Send message to a specific remote core and specific client ID on that remote core. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **hIpcNotify** IPC Notify LLD handle 
  
* **msgParams** [in] message specific structure parameter

**Return**: 

  * MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESS, message sent successfully 
  * SystemP_FAILURE, message could not be sent since HW or SW FIFO for holding the message is full. 

**Note**: 

  * To reduce latency, error checks are avoided in this API. Users need to make sure the client ID value is < [IPC_NOTIFY_CLIENT_ID_MAX](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___n_o_t_i_f_y___l_l_d___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#define-ipc_notify_client_id_max) and message value is < [IPC_NOTIFY_MSG_VALUE_MAX](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___n_o_t_i_f_y___l_l_d___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#define-ipc_notify_msg_value_max)
  * One cannot send messages to self, i.e remoteCoreId, cannot be same as core ID of the CPU that called this API.

### function IpcNotify_lld_registerClient

```
sint32 IpcNotify_lld_registerClient(
    IpcNotify_Handle hIpcNotify,
    uint16 localClientId,
    IpcNotify_FxnCallback msgCallback,
    void * args
)
```

**Brief**: 
Register a callback to handle messages received from a specific remote core and for a specific local client ID. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **hIpcNotify** IPC Notify LLD handle 
  
* **localClientId** [in] Client ID to which the message has been sent 
  
* **msgCallback** [in] Callback to invoke, if callback is already registered, error will be returned. 
  
* **args** [in] User arguments, that are passed back to user when the callback is invoked

**Return**: 

  * MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESS, callback registered sucessfully 
  * SystemP_FAILURE, callback registration failed, either remoteCoreId or localClientId is invalid or callback already registered. 

### function IpcNotify_lld_unregisterClient

```
sint32 IpcNotify_lld_unregisterClient(
    IpcNotify_Handle hIpcNotify,
    uint16 localClientId
)
```

**Brief**: 
Un-register a previously registered callback. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **hIpcNotify** IPC Notify LLD handle 
  
* **localClientId** [in] Client ID to which the message has been sent

**Return**: 

  * MCAL_SystemP_SUCCESS, callback un-registered sucessfully 
  * SystemP_FAILURE, callback un-registration failed, either remoteCoreId or localClientId is invalid 

### function __attribute__

```
__attribute__(
    (weak) 
)
```

**Brief**: 
IPC Notify LLD ISR which can be registered in the application. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **args** Argument to the ISR function. 

### function IpcNotify_lld_getSelfCoreId

```
uint32 IpcNotify_lld_getSelfCoreId(
    IpcNotify_Handle hIpcNotify
)
```

**Brief**: 
Return current core ID. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **hIpcNotify** IPC Notify LLD handle

**Return**: Core ID, see CSL_CoreID for valid values. 

### function Cdd_Ipc_Clock_ticksToUsec

```
uint32 Cdd_Ipc_Clock_ticksToUsec(
    uint32 ticks
)
```

**Brief**: 
Check if a core is enabled for IPC. 

**Parameters**: 

  
* **hIpcNotify** IPC Notify LLD handle 
  
* **coreId** [in] Core ID, see CSL_CoreID for valid values.

**Return**: 1: core is enabled for IPC, 0: core is not enabled for IPC 

### function Cdd_Ipc_Clock_uSleep

```
void Cdd_Ipc_Clock_uSleep(
    uint32 usec
)
```

### function IpcNotify_lld_isCoreEnabled

```
uint32 IpcNotify_lld_isCoreEnabled(
    IpcNotify_Handle hIpcNotify,
    uint32 coreId
)
```

# DRV_IPC_RPMESSAGE_MODULE

|typedef struct [RPMessageLLD_InitObject](../Classes/struct_r_p_message_l_l_d___init_object.md) *  | **[RPMessageLLD_InitHandle](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-rpmessagelld_inithandle)** 
|typedef struct RPMessageLLD_Object *  | **[RPMessageLLD_Handle](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-rpmessagelld_handle)** 

### typedef RPMessage_lld_recvCallback
```
typedef void(* RPMessage_lld_recvCallback) (const RPMessage_RecvCallbackParams *cbParams);
```

**Brief**: 
Callback that is invoked when a message is received from any CPU at the specified local end point. 

The callback can be optionally registered during [RPMessage_lld_construct](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#function-rpmessage_lld_construct)

**Parameters**: 

  
* **cbParams** [in] Callback parameters containing message info [RPMessage_RecvCallbackParams](../Classes/struct_r_p_message___recv_callback_params.md)

**Note**: All message contents MUST be consumed in the callback. When callback returns the message buffer is released back to the sender. If the message contents are needed for deferred processing then take a copy of the message contents

### typedef RPMessage_lld_recvNotifyCallback
```
typedef void(* RPMessage_lld_recvNotifyCallback) (void *handle, RPMessage_EpObject *obj, void *arg);
```

**Brief**: 
Callback that is invoked when a message is received from any CPU at the specified local end point. 

The callback can be optionally registered during [RPMessage_lld_construct](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#function-rpmessage_lld_construct)

**Parameters**: 

  
* **obj** [in] RPMessage end point object created with [RPMessage_lld_construct](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#function-rpmessage_lld_construct)
  
* **arg** [in] Arguments specified by user during [RPMessage_lld_construct](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#function-rpmessage_lld_construct)

**Note**: 

  * Unlike [RPMessage_lld_recvCallback](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-rpmessage_lld_recvcallback), this callback only notifies that there is one or more messages to be read, but the message itself is not read by the driver unless RPMessage_lld_recv is called in this callback or later within a task.
  * If [RPMessage_lld_recvCallback](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-rpmessage_lld_recvcallback) is set, then [RPMessage_lld_recvNotifyCallback](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#typedef-rpmessage_lld_recvnotifycallback) callback is not used.

### typedef RPMessage_ControlEndPtCallback
```
typedef void(* RPMessage_ControlEndPtCallback) (void *arg, uint16 remoteCoreId, uint16 remoteEndPt, const uint8 *remoteServiceName);
```

**Brief**: 
Callback that is invoked when a annoucement message is received on the control end point. 

The callback can be optionally registered during [RPMessage_lld_init](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#function-rpmessage_lld_init)

**Parameters**: 

  
* **arg** [in] Arguments specified by user during [RPMessage_lld_init](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#function-rpmessage_lld_init)
  
* **remoteCoreId** [in] Core ID of sender 
  
* **remoteEndPt** [in] End point of sender that has annoucned the service over the control end point 
  
* **remoteServiceName** [in] Name of the remote service that is annoucned 

**Note**: All message contents MUST be consumed in the callback. When callback returns the message buffer is released back to the sender. If the message contents are needed for deferred processing then take a copy of the message contents

### typedef RPMessageLLD_InitHandle
```
typedef  struct RPMessageLLD_InitObject * RPMessageLLD_InitHandle;
```

```
typedef  struct RPMessageLLD_Object * RPMessageLLD_Handle;

| sint32 | **[RPMessage_lld_recv](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#function-rpmessage_lld_recv)**(RPMessageLLD_Handle hRpMsg, [RPMessage_EpObject](../Classes/struct_r_p_message___ep_object.md) * epObject, [RPMessage_RecvParams](../Classes/struct_r_p_message___recv_params.md) * recvParams)|  |
| void | **[RPMessage_controlEndPtHandler](../Modules/group___d_r_v___i_p_c___r_p_m_e_s_s_a_g_e___m_o_d_u_l_e.md#function-rpmessage_controlendpthandler)**(const [RPMessage_RecvCallbackParams](../Classes/struct_r_p_message___recv_callback_params.md) * cbParams)|  |
